markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors OpenAI's GPT-6 release at 66% implied probability before GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 launch, driven by rapid AI advancements outpacing Rockstar's development timeline amid multiple delays—from Fall 2025 to May 2026, then November following Trailer 2's May 2025 drop. Recent Best Buy pre-order leaks for May 18–21 and rumors of Trailer 3 tied to Take-Two's upcoming Q4 earnings have sparked hype, yet Rockstar's focus on GTA Online updates signals continued crunch avoidance and marketing restraint. Albums from Playboi Carti or Rihanna hover near 51%, while meme bets like Jesus Christ's return or Bitcoin hitting $1M lag at 49%, underscoring uncertainty in celebrity drops and volatile markets before the blockbuster's Vice City premiere. Watch earnings for momentum shifts.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the Harvey Weinstein prison time market reflects high uncertainty amid jury deliberations in his ongoing New York rape retrial—the third for a 2013 allegation by Jessica Mann—where a conviction carries up to four years, potentially concurrent with existing sentences and overshadowed by time served since 2020. No Prison Time leads at 31% implied probability due to his age (74), recent chest pains halting proceedings, and appeals challenging a 23-year New York sexual assault sentence from 2025 plus a 16-year California rape term, where April appellate judges showed skepticism toward reversal. Close contenders like 10-20 years (25%) and 20-30 years (24%) hinge on verdict outcome and appeal resolutions, underscoring #MeToo-era judicial volatility in this landmark Hollywood reckoning. Imminent jury decision could shift dynamics sharply.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes share near-identical trader consensus at 38.7% and 37.5% implied probabilities for the 2026 Stanley Cup, reflecting their parallel dominance in the playoffs after each advanced to conference finals with gritty second-round triumphs—the Avalanche via Brett Kulak's overtime winner in a comeback against Minnesota Wild, and Hurricanes with back-to-back sweeps over Ottawa Senators and Philadelphia Flyers. This tight race underscores balanced strengths: Colorado's star-studded offense led by Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar, paired with Carolina's elite defense and depth, positioning both as consensus favorites amid the wisdom of crowds. Vegas Golden Knights (13.3%) draw on recent championship experience, while Montreal Canadiens (9.3%) and Buffalo Sabres (2.0%) vie in a heated Atlantic second-round clash as dark horses.

markets.question: Will the Colorado Avalanche win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's commanding playoff run, including a first-round sweep of the Suns and a 3-0 second-round lead over the Lakers with double-digit average margins, drives their 59.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite for the 2026 NBA Championship. San Antonio Spurs' resurgence, fueled by Victor Wembanyama's dominant performances in a 4-1 first-round win over Portland and a 3-2 conference semifinals edge against Minnesota Timberwolves after a Game 5 blowout, elevates them to 22.1%. New York Knicks' sweeps through two rounds, including a 4-0 dismissal of Philadelphia, position them at 14.1% with rest advantage awaiting the Detroit Pistons-Cleveland Cavaliers winner (Cavs lead 3-2), while lower odds reflect tougher paths and injury concerns like Detroit's questionable guards.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices France (17.3%) and Spain (16.6%) as co-favorites for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, reflecting Spain's top power ranking and unbeaten run in 90 minutes since early 2024, bolstered by a gritty 0-0 friendly draw versus Colombia last week, while France surges on Kylian Mbappé's sharp form and defensive solidity in recent internationals. England (11.3%), Brazil (9.2%), and defending champions Argentina (8.6%) lurk closely amid squad depth and attacking firepower, but Messi's age raises transition questions. The 48-team expansion, finalized qualifiers featuring powerhouses like Norway (Haaland-led) and resurgent Germany, plus balanced group draws, heighten parity and upset risks in group stage and knockouts, keeping the elite race tightly contested.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 24% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination in a fragmented field lacking an incumbent, buoyed by his national fundraising dominance, frequent media appearances critiquing Republican policies, and gubernatorial executive record in California through early 2027. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez surging to 26% on progressive appeal and youth among primary voters, while former Vice President Kamala Harris lingers at 9% amid lingering 2024 campaign scrutiny. This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 24% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination in a fragmented field lacking an incumbent, buoyed by his national fundraising dominance, frequent media appearances critiquing Republican policies, and gubernatorial executive record in California through early 2027. Recent AtlasIntel polling shows Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez surging to 26% on progressive appeal and youth among primary voters, while former Vice President Kamala Harris lingers at 9% amid lingering 2024 campaign scrutiny. Key differentiators include Newsom's centrist appeal to donors and swing-state viability versus Harris's high name recognition but electoral baggage, and Ocasio-Cortez's grassroots energy offset by limited executive experience. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm outcomes elevating governors like Shapiro or Beshear, major endorsements, or early fundraising filings.
